target: F64 clamp with scalar bounds (clamp(min, x, max) and legacy (x, min, max) orders)

lever.description: Add clamp_f64_scalar_bounds fast path to eval_clamp's two scalar-bounds tensor arms: when x is an F64 tensor and both bounds are F64Bits scalars, extract lo/hi once and loop clamp_f64 directly, skipping the per-element clamp_literal 3-tuple match and bound re-extraction. Returns None (fall through) for non-F64 inputs.

baseline.note: fast-path guards temporarily removed; generic per-element clamp_literal path

after.tool: rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- 'eval/clamp_1k_f64'

isomorphism_proof.ordering: Output elements produced in tensor index order; identical clamp per position.
isomorphism_proof.floating_point: Calls the same clamp_f64 the generic path uses for all-F64Bits operands; clamp_literal(F64,F64,F64) = from_f64(clamp_f64(...)), an exact match.
isomorphism_proof.tie_breaking: None introduced; NaN propagation (any-NaN -> canonical NaN) and -0.0 preserved (golden indices 3,4).
isomorphism_proof.rng: No RNG state is read or written.
isomorphism_proof.errors: Only F64 tensor x with F64 scalar bounds takes the fast path; other dtypes, tensor bounds, and malformed literals use the generic path.
isomorphism_proof.unit_test: fj-lax arithmetic::tests::clamp_f64_tensor_scalar_bounds_fast_path_bit_identical (both operand orders; NaN/-0.0/+-inf); 996/996 fj-lax tests pass.
